title: Add AppArmor key to quadlet containers
body:
This addresses #27095 by adding an AppArmor key to `.container` quadlet files.

#### Checklist

Ensure you have completed the following checklist for your pull request to be reviewed:
<!-- Use [x] to mark as done, or click the checkbox after opening PR -->

- [ x ] Certify you wrote the patch or otherwise have the right to pass it on as an open-source patch by signing all
commits. (`git commit -s`). (If needed, use `git commit -s --amend`).  The author email must match
the sign-off email address. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-your-prs)
for more information.
- [ x ] Referenced issues using `Fixes: #00000` in commit message (if applicable)
- [ x ] [Tests](https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/test#readme) have been added/updated (or no tests are needed)
- [ x ] [Documentation](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/README.md) has been updated (or no documentation changes are needed)
- [ x ] All commits pass `make validatepr` (format/lint checks)
- [ x ] [Release note](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/guide/release-notes.md) entered in the section below (or `None` if no user-facing changes)

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
Add AppArmor key to quadlet `.container` files which equates to `--security-opt apparmor=` flag.
```

comments.body: [NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.
Test failures are fixed on main - please rebase and we should be good to merge
CI failures are unrelated to this PR and fixed on main; please rebase, and that should clear things for merge.

> @givensuman please squash (fixup) the commits into one as mentioned in [#27420 (review)](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27420#pullrequestreview-3412415011), we like a proper git history with useful commit messages. And as @mheon mentioned you will need to rebase as well on main to make the CI happy.
> 
> If you don't know how to squash and rebase I am happy to do it or give you instructions on how to.

Lemme know if that did the trick!

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *givensuman*, *Luap99*, *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99,mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Just tried troubleshooting why `AppArmor` wasn't recognized with Podman 5.7.1 (_the latest version currently released_) despite coming across it in the [`latest` documentation](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-systemd.unit.5.html#container-units-container) (_but not the [`v5.7.1` versioned docs](https://docs.podman.io/en/v5.7.1/markdown/podman-systemd.unit.5.html#container-units-container)_).

I assume `latest` at the docs doesn't align with the latest Podman release but reflects the upcoming release? (_**EDIT:** [`latest` refers to the `main` branch](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/Reference.html), while `stable` presumably refers to "latest stable" release [indicated at the main website](https://podman.io/)_)

- This PR was merged in Nov 2025, but [5.7.1 was released in Dec 2025](https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/tag/v5.7.1) and [5.7.0 days after this PR was merged](https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/tag/v5.7.0). So `latest` doesn't seem to align with that, is this change scheduled for `5.8`/`6.0`?
- I noticed the [README directing me to the roadmap](https://github.com/containers/podman#podman-information-for-developers) for what would presumably indicate this change.
  - Presently the [`main` branch roadmap linked](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/RELEASE_NOTES.md) is for [a commit from 5 months ago](https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/a5a00c1796ad56f729220caa550dd56b958bcc7c) with only 5.6.0 as the latest release, despite 5.7.0 + 5.7.1 already being released?
  - [Release Notes @ v5.7.1 tag](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v5.7.1/RELEASE_NOTES.md) does cover 5.7.0 + 5.7.1.

Apologies if this information is explained somewhere, I don't see any context in this PR or the other sources I've cited for when this feature is expected to land?

This landed after the 5.7 RC1 feature freeze in late October '25, so it is not in 5.7. It will be in the mid-February 5.8 release. We do not document our exact RC policy IIRC, but all RCs are considered fully stable and ready to release, and as such we do not allow new features in after we begin cutting RCs.

No worries, thanks for chiming in about that ❤️ 

I was mostly just curious about how to identify where to track such additions (_since the `main` branch roadmap was already outdated from the `5.7.x` series, which was a tad confusing_).

I've sometimes seen a changelog or roadmap with an unreleased section at the top that upon release is changed to a concrete version (_with a new unreleased header taking it's place above it_). That or a version tag/label that has now been added (_but might not always be predictable_), there's also the Github milestones feature which can help group associated issues/tasks.
